architecture_summary: Add a read-only Foundation Mode status read model that consumes the runtime closure summary and exposes the operator-facing state: evidence accepted, admission denied, promotion denied, live runtime blocked, and Foundation closure complete.
data_model_changes: Introduce a status read-model schema and fixture with explicit projection scope, operator status, runtime status, authority denials, chain refs, evidence refs, and reconciled counts.
Preserve the source Foundation closure summary while adding a concise operator-facing projection.
Register the schema in the protocol manifest, schema index, CI worker lane, docs, and SDLC artifacts.
implementation_plan: Add status read-model schema, fixture, validator, and focused tests.
Register the schema in the protocol manifest and CI worker runtime enablement lane.
Update worker docs, schema README, gap register, platform overview, and SDLC artifacts.
Run focused validator, focused pytest, protocol manifest validation, SDLC artifact validation, schema validation, and whitespace checks.
invariants: Read model is projection-only and does not execute runtime.
Foundation closure is not live terminal closure.
Runtime admission, promotion, enablement, dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and success claims remain denied.
The status answer is derived from the Foundation closure summary.
rollback_plan: Remove status read-model schema, fixture, validator, tests, manifest entry, CI inclusion, docs updates, and SDLC artifacts.
Restore the worker runtime chain to relying directly on the Foundation closure summary.
tradeoffs: The slice adds an operator projection without runtime authority.
Future live runtime authority still requires a separate non-Foundation thread.
